Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex This attack involves a sophisticated multi-stage process targeting water and wastewater systems, utilizing various techniques to gather information, establish persistence, and disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Process File Active Directory Scheduled Task User Account Windows Service Network Traffic Network Share DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility File Creation (Mechanism) File Modification (Mechanism) Custom Command and Control Protocol Scheduled Task/Job Process Creation (Mechanism) File Metadata (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about water and wastewater systems using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies potential entry points and network architecture by monitoring network traffic.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker creates custom tools to exploit vulnerabilities by obtaining decommissioned systems and devices.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker uses publicly known vulnerabilities in SCADA systems to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker compromises employee devices through phishing emails.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attacker establishes command and control channels for further actions.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to run at specific times.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker installs malware on system images.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: The attacker creates web shells for remote access.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker elevates privileges using compromised credentials.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker impersonates system administrators.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-702: Exploiting Incorrect Chaining or Granularity of Hardware Debug Components
stage.description: The attacker prevents detection by anti-debugging techniques.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker obfuscates malicious code.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker cracks passwords using brute force attacks.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker captures login credentials.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps out network infrastructure.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker discovers running processes.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally through remote services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses valid accounts to move laterally.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data.

stage.name: Collection
stage.technique_name: T1039: Data from Network Shared Drive
stage.capec: CAPEC-643: Identify Shared Files/Directories on System
stage.description: The attacker collects data from network traffic.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker transfers files for exfiltration.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical operations.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker destroys sensitive data.

Scenario.summary: This complex attack targets water and wastewater systems, involving multiple stages of reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
